steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says fish studies showed larger differences on average than bird studies, but in every animal class — including birds — some individual studies exceeded the fish average. The question asks what can be inferred.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the studies in the meta-analysis that examined fish were more likely than those that examined birds to specify whether the observed effects were detrimental.’: introduces a detail not supported by the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘the differences that studies attribute to exposure to anthropogenic noise are likely to be more pronounced for birds than they are for fish.’: contradicts the on-average finding for fish. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘the difference found in the study conducted by Lidia Eva Wysocki and colleagues was likely larger than the average difference for studies of common carp included in the meta-analysis.’: makes an unsupported claim about Wysocki's specific study. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘some studies of birds found larger effects of exposure to anthropogenic noise than some studies of fish did.’: if some bird studies exceeded the fish average, they necessarily exceeded fish studies that fell below that average. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
